2026-04-18T18:03:08.9606923Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-18T18:03:08.9607489Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-18T18:03:08.9607915Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-18T18:03:08.9608291Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-18T18:03:08.9608954Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-18T18:03:08.9609210Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-18T18:03:08.9609375Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-18T18:03:08.9609558Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-18T18:03:08.9609725Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-18T18:03:08.9609955Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-18T18:03:08.9610156Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-18T18:03:08.9610444Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-18T18:03:08.9610716Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

2026-04-18T18:06:04.4940236Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-18T18:04:15.517727182Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58330,\"title\":\"[Azure Management Generator] Docs: Missing Usage/Prerequisites section in http-client-csharp-mgmt README\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt`\\n**Service directory:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #58323 by `@JoshLove-msft`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #58323 added `regen-docs` and `regen-docs:only` npm scripts to both `http-client-csharp` and `http-client-csharp-mgmt` packages, and integrated doc regeneration into `Generate.ps1`. As part of this, the `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md` was auto-regenerated using `tspd doc`. However, the `tspd` tool only regenerates emitter option documentation — it does not preserve or generate the human-authored `## Usage` section.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md` is missing the `## Usage` section (with `### Prerequisite` and `### Customizing Generated Code` sub-sections) that exists in the parallel `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/README.md`\\n- The Prerequisites sub-section (Node.js and .NET SDK version requirements) is absent, leaving mgmt emitter users without setup guidance\\n- There is no link to a Customization Guide for mgmt-specific generated code (comparable to the one in the data-plane README)\\n- Neither README documents the new `regen-docs` and `regen-docs:only` npm scripts — developers modifying emitter options manually need to know they can run `npm run regen-docs` to regenerate the README\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md`\\n\\n**Edit** — Insert the following `## Usage` section after the `## Install` section and before the `## Emitter usage` section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Usage\\n\\n### Prerequisite\\n\\n- Install [Node.js]((nodejs.org/redacted) 20 or above. (Verify by running `node --version`)\\n- Install [**.NET 10.0 SDK**]((dotnet.microsoft.com/redacted) for your specific platform. (or a higher version within the 10.0.\\\\* band)\\n\\n### Customizing Generated Code\\n\\nFor detailed instructions on how to customize the generated C# code for Azure management libraries, see the [Customization Guide](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/emitter.md).\\n\\n### Regenerating Documentation\\n\\nThe emitter option documentation in this README is auto-generated. To regenerate it after making changes to emitter options, run:\\n\\n```\\nnpm run regen-docs\\n```\\n\\nThis rebuilds the emitter and then regenerates all option documentation using `tspd`. When running `Generate.ps1`, docs are regenerated automatically via `regen-docs:only` (without rebuilding the emitter).\\n```\\n\\n#### File: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/README.md`\\n\\n**Edit** — Add a \\u0026#34;Regenerating Documentation\\u0026#34; sub-section under `## Usage`, after `### Customizing Generated Code`:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Regenerating Documentation\\n\\nThe emitter option documentation in this README is auto-generated. To regenerate it after making changes to emitter options, run:\\n\\n```\\nnpm run regen-docs\\n```\\n\\nThis rebuilds the emitter and then regenerates all option documentation using `tspd`. When running `Generate.ps1`, docs are regenerated automatically via `regen-docs:only` (without rebuilding the emitter).\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNot applicable — these are tool/generator package READMEs, not Azure SDK client library packages. No snippet test files apply.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md` should contain these sections after the fix:\\n1. `## Install` ✅ (already present)\\n2. `## Usage` ❌ **missing** — add it\\n   - `### Prerequisite`\\n   - `### Customizing Generated Code`\\n   - `### Regenerating Documentation`\\n3. `## Emitter usage` ✅ (already present)\\n4. `## Emitter options` ✅ (already present, auto-generated)\\n\\nReference: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/README.md` for section formatting and tone.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order from the repository root:\\n\\n1. Verify the README changes look correct manually — no build is needed for pure documentation edits to these generator packages\\n2. Optionally, to confirm `regen-docs` still works after the edit (note: `tspd` only regenerates the `## Emitter options` section, so the `## Usage` section added manually will be preserved):\\n   ```powershell\\n   cd eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt\\n   npm install\\n   npm run regen-docs\\n   ```\\n   Check that the `## Usage` section is still present in the regenerated file (it should be, since `tspd` only affects the emitter options sections it generates).\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!WARNING]\\n\\u0026gt; \\n\\u0026gt; \\u003cstrong\\u003e⚠️ Firewall blocked 2 domains\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; The following domains were blocked by the firewall during workflow execution:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; - `pkgs.dev.azure.com`\\n\\u0026gt; - `releaseassets.githubusercontent.com`\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; ```yaml\\n\\u0026gt; network:\\n\\u0026gt;   allowed:\\n\\u0026gt;     - defaults\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;pkgs.dev.azure.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;releaseassets.githubusercontent.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt; ```\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; See [Network Configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/network/) for more information.\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; \\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24610489638/agentic_workflow) · ● 768.6K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22\\u0026amp;type=issues)\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58330\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"github-actions[bot]\",\"id\":41898282,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/apps/github-actions\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":0,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":0},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-18T18:01:42Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-18T18:01:42Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

As part of this, the `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md` was auto-regenerated using `tspd doc`. However, the `tspd` tool only regenerates emitter option documentation — it does not preserve or generate the human-authored `## Usage` section.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md` is missing the `## Usage` section (with `### Prerequisite` and `### Customizing Generated Code` sub-sections) that exists in the parallel `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/README.md`\\n- The Prerequisites sub-section (Node.js and .NET SDK version requirements) is absent, leaving mgmt emitter users without setup guidance\\n- There is no link to a Customization Guide for mgmt-specific generated code (comparable to the one in the data-plane README)\\n- Neither README documents the new `regen-docs` and `regen-docs:only` npm scripts — developers modifying emitter options manually need to know they can run `npm run regen-docs` to regenerate the README\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md`\\n\\n**Edit** — Insert the following `## Usage` section after the `## Install` section and before the `## Emitter usage` section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Usage\\n\\n### Prerequisite\\n\\n- Install [Node.js]((nodejs.org/redacted) 20 or above. (Verify by running `node --version`)\\n- Install [**.NET 10.0 SDK**]((dotnet.microsoft.com/redacted) for your specific platform. (or a higher version within the 10.0.\\\\* band)\\n\\n### Customizing Generated Code\\n\\nFor detailed instructions on how to customize the generated C# code for Azure management libraries, see the [Customization Guide](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/emitter.md).\\n\\n### Regenerating Documentation\\n\\nThe emitter option documentation in this README is auto-generated. To regenerate it after making changes to emitter options, run:\\n\\n```\\nnpm run regen-docs\\n```\\n\\nThis rebuilds the emitter and then regenerates all option documentation using `tspd`. When running `Generate.ps1`, docs are regenerated automatically via `regen-docs:only` (without rebuilding the emitter).\\n```\\n\\n#### File: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/README.md`\\n\\n**Edit** — Add a \\u0026#34;Regenerating Documentation\\u0026#34; sub-section under `## Usage`, after `### Customizing Generated Code`:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Regenerating Documentation\\n\\nThe emitter option documentation in this README is auto-generated. To regenerate it after making changes to emitter options, run:\\n\\n```\\nnpm run regen-docs\\n```\\n\\nThis rebuilds the emitter and then regenerates all option documentation using `tspd`. When running `Generate.ps1`, docs are regenerated automatically via `regen-docs:only` (without rebuilding the emitter).\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNot applicable — these are tool/generator package READMEs, not Azure SDK client library packages. No snippet test files apply.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md` should contain these sections after the fix:\\n1. `## Install` ✅ (already present)\\n2. `## Usage` ❌ **missing** — add it\\n   - `### Prerequisite`\\n   - `### Customizing Generated Code`\\n   - `### Regenerating Documentation`\\n3. `## Emitter usage` ✅ (already present)\\n4. `## Emitter options` ✅ (already present, auto-generated)\\n\\nReference: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/README.md` for section formatting and tone.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order from the repository root:\\n\\n1. Verify the README changes look correct manually — no build is needed for pure documentation edits to these generator packages\\n2. Optionally, to confirm `regen-docs` still works after the edit (note: `tspd` only regenerates the `## Emitter options` section, so the `## Usage` section added manually will be preserved):\\n   ```powershell\\n   cd eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt\\n   npm install\\n   npm run regen-docs\\n   ```\\n   Check that the `## Usage` section is still present in the regenerated file (it should be, since `tspd` only affects the emitter options sections it generates).\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt;
